findings.message: Body is 5 lines with no examples, tools, or hooks; provides almost no behavioral guidance beyond a single sentence.

`.claude/mcp.json` registers only the `flow-nexus` MCP server. All agent hooks referencing `mcp__claude-flow__*` tools (swarm, memory, neural, performance) require the claude-flow MCP server, which is not configured here. Agents break silently on a fresh clone.

Root CLAUDE.md states '16 agent roles + custom types' in one place and '60+ agent types' in another. Audit found 84 agent files (~64 unique after deduplication). Neither count matches the actual state.
`name: analyst` in frontmatter conflicts with CLAUDE.md registry entry `code-analyzer` and body title; Claude Code will route `@code-analyzer` to the wrong file.

File is an exact duplicate of `v3/@claude-flow/cli/.claude/agents/analysis/analyze-code-quality.md`; ~20 such duplicate pairs exist across the repo (nested `backend/`, `ci-cd/`, `unit/`, etc. subdirs mirror parent dirs).
No `model` field in frontmatter; all 5 core agents (coder, reviewer, tester, planner, researcher) are missing model declarations.
All 9 flow-nexus agents missing output_format; agents invoke MCP tools but never declare what structured response they return.
Hook calls undefined bash function `calculate_review_quality`; post-task learning never fires for issue review work.
Hooks call undefined bash functions `calculate_pr_success` and `validate_pr_merge`; these are not shell built-ins and have no definition in scope, so post-task tracking silently drops.
Hook calls undefined bash function `calculate_release_quality`; release-quality metrics are never stored.

Optimization agents (benchmark-suite, performance-monitor, load-balancer, resource-allocator, topology-optimizer) all hit the vague-quantifier cap; bodies are JS class stubs with no specific behavioral thresholds.

Benchmark-suite agent body is entirely JavaScript class stubs with no agent instructions, no output contract, and no hooks. Frontmatter has `name`, `type`, `category`, `description` but no behavioral content.
Pre hook calls `memory_store` and `memory_search` as bare bash commands; both are undefined shell functions.
Pre hook calls `memory_store` as a bare bash command; undefined function causes silent failure.
Pre hook calls `memory_store` as a bare bash command; undefined function causes silent failure.
Pre hook calls `memory_store "sparc_phase" "specification"` — `memory_store` is not a shell command; SPARC phase tracking silently drops on every invocation.

SPARC agents use `npx claude-flow@alpha memory search-patterns` (v2 subcommand) while core agents use `npx claude-flow@v3alpha`. The v2 `search-patterns` subcommand does not exist in v3; SPARC hook memory lookups silently fail.

Pre and post hooks invoke `mcp__claude-flow__swarm_init`, `mcp__claude-flow__neural_patterns`, `mcp__claude-flow__model_save` etc. as bash commands; MCP tool identifiers are not executables and will fail with command-not-found on every hook trigger.
No `model` field in frontmatter; agent cannot be routed to an appropriate Claude tier by the 3-tier ADR-026 routing system.
Zero frontmatter `examples:` blocks; all 82 non-arch agents omit example input/output pairs, making behavioral validation from metadata impossible.
No `output_format` field in frontmatter; present across all 82 non-arch agents uniformly.
Vague quantifiers at cap: 'intelligent', 'optimal', 'appropriate', 'dynamic', 'adaptive', 'efficiently' appear 10+ times.
Hooks invoke `mcp__claude-flow__swarm_init hierarchical`, `mcp__claude-flow__swarm_monitor`, `mcp__claude-flow__coordination_sync` as bash commands; all will fail with command-not-found.
Hooks invoke `mcp__claude-flow__swarm_init mesh`, `mcp__claude-flow__daa_communication`, `mcp__claude-flow__daa_consensus` as bash commands; all will fail.
No `model` field in frontmatter; affects all 7 consensus agents uniformly.
Zero frontmatter examples; consensus agent bodies contain JS implementation classes rather than behavioral guidance or illustrative use cases.
Vague quantifiers at cap: 'appropriate', 'optimal', 'intelligent', 'efficient', 'properly' appear 10+ times across the file body.
Post hook uses `$(mcp__claude-flow__swarm_status | jq -r '.topology')` as a bash subshell; the MCP tool name is not an executable, so jq receives no input and memory stores an empty string.
Hooks call undefined bash functions `calculate_pr_success` and `validate_pr_merge`; these are not shell built-ins and have no definition in scope, so post-task tracking silently drops.
Hook calls undefined bash function `calculate_review_quality`; post-task learning never fires for issue review work.
Hook calls undefined bash function `calculate_release_quality`; release-quality metrics are never stored.
Pre hook calls `memory_store "sparc_phase" "specification"` — `memory_store` is not a shell command; SPARC phase tracking silently drops on every invocation.
Pre hook calls `memory_store` and `memory_search` as bare bash commands; both are undefined shell functions.
Pre hook calls `memory_store` as a bare bash command; undefined function causes silent failure.
Pre hook calls `memory_store` as a bare bash command; undefined function causes silent failure.

Pre and post hooks invoke `mcp__claude-flow__swarm_init`, `mcp__claude-flow__neural_patterns`, `mcp__claude-flow__model_save` etc. as bash commands; MCP tool identifiers are not executables and will fail with command-not-found on every hook trigger.
Hooks invoke `mcp__claude-flow__swarm_init hierarchical`, `mcp__claude-flow__swarm_monitor`, `mcp__claude-flow__coordination_sync` as bash commands; all will fail with command-not-found.
Hooks invoke `mcp__claude-flow__swarm_init mesh`, `mcp__claude-flow__daa_communication`, `mcp__claude-flow__daa_consensus` as bash commands; all will fail.
Post hook uses `$(mcp__claude-flow__swarm_status | jq -r '.topology')` as a bash subshell; the MCP tool name is not an executable, so jq receives no input and memory stores an empty string.

No `model` field in frontmatter; agent cannot be routed to an appropriate Claude tier by the 3-tier ADR-026 routing system.
No `model` field in frontmatter; affects all 7 consensus agents uniformly.
No `model` field in frontmatter; all 5 core agents (coder, reviewer, tester, planner, researcher) are missing model declarations.
Zero frontmatter `examples:` blocks; all 82 non-arch agents omit example input/output pairs, making behavioral validation from metadata impossible.
Zero frontmatter examples; consensus agent bodies contain JS implementation classes rather than behavioral guidance or illustrative use cases.
No `output_format` field in frontmatter; present across all 82 non-arch agents uniformly.

Vague quantifiers at cap: 'appropriate', 'optimal', 'intelligent', 'efficient', 'properly' appear 10+ times across the file body.
